encounter.reason: 48-hour Holter monitor study to assess atrial fibrillation burden, rate control adequacy, and ventricular ectopy
encounter.service_provider: University of Minnesota Cardiac Electrophysiology Clinic
encounter.location: University of Minnesota Cardiac Electrophysiology Clinic, Minneapolis, MN

vitals.name: average ventricular rate during Holter monitoring
minimum ventricular rate during Holter monitoring
maximum ventricular rate during Holter monitoring
vitals.context: occurred during brisk walking at 10:42 on 2026-02-19
vitals.interpretation: brisk exertional response but within acceptable limits

vitals.name: atrial fibrillation burden
vitals.interpretation: persistent atrial fibrillation confirmed; no periods of sinus rhythm identified

vitals.name: longest R-R interval (longest pause)
vitals.interpretation: no pauses greater than 2.0 seconds; not clinically significant

vitals.name: premature ventricular complex (PVC) burden
vitals.interpretation: rare isolated PVCs, no couplets, no ventricular tachycardia runs; low arrhythmic risk

vitals.name: total Holter recording duration
vitals.interpretation: adequate recording duration, signal quality adequate throughout

vitals.name: predominant cardiac rhythm on Holter monitoring
vitals.value: atrial fibrillation (continuous, no sinus rhythm identified)
vitals.interpretation: persistent atrial fibrillation throughout entire recording

vitals.name: number of patient-reported cardiac events during Holter monitoring
vitals.context: all correlated with periods of higher ventricular rate (88-118 bpm) during atrial fibrillation; no associated bradycardia, pauses, or ventricular ectopy at event times

conditions.name: persistent atrial fibrillation
conditions.note: Holter results confirm reclassification from atrial fibrillation to persistent atrial fibrillation based on 100% AF burden with no periods of sinus rhythm over 48 hours

document_references.type: diagnostic report document / Holter monitor interpretive report
document_references.description: Complete 48-hour Holter monitor study report including indication, device details, rhythm analysis, ventricular rate summary, pause analysis, ectopy analysis, patient-reported event correlation, ST-segment analysis, and final impression by Dr. James Lindgren confirming persistent atrial fibrillation with adequate rate control

Brief note documenting Holter monitor application by Maria Torres, RN. Includes device configuration (3-channel, 7-lead), electrode placement verification, patient education on symptom diary use, event marker button instructions, and activity guidance.

Brief note documenting Holter monitor removal. Patient reported 4 event marker activations during the monitoring period (twice during walks, twice during household activities). No device malfunction or skin irritation noted.
